title: Technology Basics
description: Broad, feed-friendly technology questions for everyday digital literacy.

questions.promptText: What does a CPU mainly do in a computer?
questions.explanationText: The CPU is the main processor that executes instructions and coordinates computing tasks.
RAM holds short-term data that active apps and processes need right now.

questions.promptText: Which storage type uses flash memory and has no moving parts?
questions.explanationText: An SSD uses flash storage, so it is typically faster and more shock-resistant than a spinning hard drive.

questions.promptText: What does HTTPS protect on a website visit?
questions.explanationText: HTTPS encrypts the data exchanged with the site, even though the domain itself may still be visible.

questions.promptText: What is a cloud service in simple terms?
questions.explanationText: Cloud services provide computing resources over the internet instead of only from your local device.
options: A service delivered over the internet

questions.explanationText: Machine learning is an AI approach where systems learn patterns from data rather than following only hand-written rules.
Inference is the stage where a trained model produces a prediction or output from new input.
options: The output stage of a trained model

questions.explanationText: Phishing is a scam that tricks people into revealing information through fake messages, websites, or logins.

questions.promptText: What does multi-factor authentication add to a login?
questions.explanationText: Multi-factor authentication requires more than one type of proof, which makes account takeover harder.

questions.promptText: What is the internet best described as?
questions.explanationText: The internet is a global network of connected computer networks that exchange data using common protocols.
